farmer: "Another day in the fields. The {season} sun is {weatherComment}."
"Ah, a stranger. Don't see many new faces at planting time. What brings you to {location}?"
"The paddies {paddyState}. My back aches, but the land provides."
"Careful where you step — the irrigation channels are slippery this time of year."
"You look hungry. There's still some millet porridge left if you want."
"Rain would be welcome. The seedlings are thirsty."
"Last harvest was poor, but the kami have blessed us with a mild winter so far."
"Have you come to buy rice? The price is high this month."
"My eldest son left to join the levy. I pray he returns."
"The scarecrows need repairing. Always something to do."
merchant: "Welcome, welcome! Have a look at my wares. Finest {goods} in {location}!"
"Business has been {businessState} lately. Not many caravans from {tradeRoute} since the {event}."
"A shrewd buyer, I can tell. Let's talk price."
"I just received a shipment of {localSpecialty}. You won't find better quality."
"Coin is tight this season. If you have something to trade, I'm listening."
"Everything is negotiable. Name your offer."
"I have contacts in Kamakura, Edo, and even the capital. I can get anything."
"Careful of the counterfeit coins going around. I check every piece."
"Travel is dangerous now. Half my usual suppliers haven't arrived."
"A good merchant knows when to hold and when to sell. Today, I sell."
monk: "Amida Buddha watches over all who walk the path. Welcome, traveller."
"The temple bells ring for the departed. Have you come to pray?"
"Sit with me. The world's troubles can wait a moment."
"I sense a restlessness in you. Perhaps a cup of tea will soothe it."
"The leaves are changing. A reminder that all things are impermanent."
"Compassion is the only true weapon against suffering."
"We hold a meditation at dusk. You are welcome to join."
"The abbot speaks of a coming storm — not of weather, but of men's hearts."
"If you carry a burden, leave it at the temple gate."
"Even the most fearsome warrior will one day be dust. Reflect on that."
samurai: "State your business. The {clan} clan does not tolerate idle wanderers."
"Your blade — it looks well‑used. Have you seen battle?"
"My lord requires {lordDemand}. These are troubled times, and loyalty is everything."
"I remember the fields of {battlefield}. The cries still echo."
"If you serve no master, you serve only yourself. That is a dangerous path."
"Stand straight. A warrior's posture reflects his spirit."
"I am on patrol. Move along unless you have business here."
"The code of bushidō is all that separates us from beasts."
"My sword has tasted blood. Pray it does not taste yours."
"A samurai's word is his bond. Remember that."
blacksmith: "The forge is hot and the iron waits for no one. What do you need?"
"A new blade? Repairs? I can shape anything from a plowshare to a katana."
"Iron ore from Hitachi is getting scarce. Prices may rise."
"Mind the sparks — that's good steel folding under the hammer."
"They say a well‑forged sword carries the soul of its maker. This one's almost ready."
"I learned this trade from my father. He learned from his."
"Good tools make all the difference. A dull axe is a danger to everyone."
"If you bring me raw iron, I'll give you a discount on the work."
"Quenching is an art. Too fast, the blade shatters. Too slow, it's soft."
"I don't just make weapons. Nails, hinges, cooking pots — all essential."
innkeeper: "A room for the night? The hearth is warm and the sake is cold."
"You're in luck — the last room is yours, if you have the coin."
"Travellers have been few since the {event}. It's hard times."
"Supper is rice and grilled fish. Not fancy, but it fills the belly."
"Watch your belongings. There have been reports of petty thieves."
"The bath is out back. Hot water costs extra."
"I can stable your horse for the night. Extra fodder is two mon."
"If you need directions, just ask. I know every road in the province."
"We don't allow fighting inside. Take it outside."
"A group of merchants came through yesterday. They paid in silver."
fisherman: "The sea was rough this morning. Barely a catch to speak of."
"Best bonito you'll taste this side of Kazusa. Fresh off the boat."
"The tide's coming in. Good time to try your luck with a line."
"There's a storm brewing. Smell the air? We're in for a blow."
"A kappa was spotted near the river mouth. Keep your wits about you."
"My grandfather fished these waters. His grandfather before him."
"I lost a net to a shark last week. Costly business."
"The best fishing is at dawn, when the sea is glass."
"We offer prayers to Ebisu-sama before every voyage."
"You can buy dried fish from me for half what the market charges."
woodcutter: "The forest has been good this year. Plenty of oak and cedar."
"Listen — do you hear that? A woodpecker. Good omen."
"Charcoal burning is hard work, but it keeps the village warm in winter."
"Stay on the path if you go into the woods. Wolves have been seen."
"My axe is getting dull. Time to see the blacksmith."
"Sometimes I see strange lights in the trees at night. The monks say it's nothing."
"Timber prices are up. More building in the towns."
"I once saw a bear as big as a horse. I ran so fast I left my sandals behind."
"The mountain kami are real. Show respect when you take from their forest."
"I've been cutting wood here for thirty years. It's honest work."
beggar: "Spare a mon for a poor soul? The war took everything."
"I used to be a farmer, before the flood. Now I have nothing."
"The temple gives out rice on festival days. Small mercies."
"You look like a kind soul. May the kami bless your journey."
"The nights are cold. Even a scrap of cloth would help."
"I can tell you the gossip of the town. I hear everything."
"Don't pity me. I was once a proud man."
"The children throw stones, but I'm used to it."
"If you give me something, I'll pray for your fortune."
"Even a dying dog can still bite. I'm not completely useless."
entertainer: "A song? A dance? I can play the biwa or spin a tale for your amusement."
"The capital's the place for real entertainment. Out here, we make do."
"I heard a rumour that a Noh troupe is travelling through Musashi. Perhaps we'll see them."
"For a few mon, I'll tell you a story that'll make your heart race."
"Laughter is the best medicine, they say. Try it — it's free."
"I used to perform at the court of a daimyō. Then the war came."
"My biwa has seen better days. The strings are old, but the sound is still sweet."
"Every night I perform, someone buys me a drink. Tonight, it's your turn."
"I know a hundred stories, from the creation of the world to yesterday's battle."
"If you have a story worth telling, I'll trade you a song for it."
doctor: "You look pale. Have you been eating enough?"
"I can prepare a remedy for that cough. Willow bark and ginger."
"Acupuncture can relieve many pains. Lie down and relax."
"This poultice will draw out the infection. Change it twice a day."
"The body's humours must stay balanced. Too much fire leads to fever."
"I studied under a Chinese physician who knew secrets lost to most."
"Some illnesses are of the spirit, not the body. Have you consulted a priest?"
"Rest is the best medicine. But nobody listens to that advice."
"I charge according to what the patient can afford. The wealthy pay for the poor."
"If you bring me herbs, I'll treat you for free."

farmer: "Did you hear? Old man {npcName} found a buried jar of coins in his field. Lucky devil."
"The headman's daughter ran off with a peddler. Scandalous!"
"They say the rice tax is going up again. The Shogun's men are relentless."
"A white fox was seen near the shrine. That's either a blessing or a curse."
"My neighbour's cow gave birth to a two‑headed calf. Bad omen, that."
"The smith's apprentice has been sneaking out at night. Meeting someone, I'd wager."
"I heard a baby crying in the woods. Probably just a fox, but still..."
"A stranger was asking about the old battlefield. Treasure hunter?"
"The river has risen three feet since last week. Floods might be coming."
"My wife saw a shooting star last night and made a wish. I hope it was for a good harvest."
merchant: "Word is, a large caravan from the capital was attacked on the Tōkaidō. Bandits grow bolder."
"Silk prices are falling — a ship from Ming China docked in Sakai."
"The Satake and Uesugi are massing troops. War is coming."
"A new coin is being minted. It's debased, they say, but it spends the same."
"Someone in {location} is hoarding salt. Price spike coming, mark my words."
"The innkeeper overcharges strangers. I've seen his ledgers."
"A wealthy widow from Kamakura is looking to invest in a trading venture."
"They say the magistrate takes bribes. That's why certain cases never go to trial."
"The price of charcoal will double before winter. Buy now if you can."
"A rival merchant tried to undercut me. He's now selling rotten fish down by the docks."
monk: "A hermit in the mountains claims to have seen the Buddha in a waterfall."
"Pilgrims from Awa spoke of a great fish that sang like a woman."
"A tengu was sighted in Nikkō. The yamabushi are preparing a purification ritual."
"The abbot is ill. They say it's a curse from a disgruntled spirit."
"A wandering nun performed a miracle — healed a child with just a touch."
"The temple records speak of a hidden treasure buried during the Genpei War."
"I heard a voice in the meditation hall last night. No one else was there."
"The cherry tree in the courtyard bloomed out of season. A sign from the heavens."
"A samurai came to confess his sins. He left without his sword."
"The novice monk has dreams that come true. The abbot fears for his safety."
samurai: "The Chiba clan is recruiting ronin. They're offering good pay."
"A duel took place at the crossroads yesterday. Two samurai, both dead."
"My lord has received a secret envoy from the Southern Court. Treason or diplomacy?"
"One of the castle guards vanished last night. His armour was found by the river."
"A famous swordmaster is passing through {location}. Every warrior wants to challenge him."
"I overheard my lord arguing with the tax collector. Voices were raised."
"The daimyō's son is a coward. He'll never lead men into battle."
"They say a ronin in the hills has mastered a technique that can cut through stone."
"The Shogun's inspectors are travelling incognito. They could be anyone."
"An old woman spat at a samurai and he did nothing. He must be disgraced."
commoner: "Have you seen the new shrine gate? It was funded by a wealthy merchant."
"A fire broke out in the lower quarter. Three houses lost."
"The midwife says the baker's wife is pregnant with twins."
"Someone's been stealing turnips from my garden. I blame the children."
"The village council is meeting tonight about the new irrigation ditch."
"I saw a dog walking on its hind legs. My grandmother says that's bad luck."
"The constable has been drinking more than usual. Something's troubling him."
"A peddler is selling miracle cures. I'd call them snake oil, but people buy them."
"The temple bell rang by itself yesterday. Everyone's spooked."
"The rice merchant's wife left him. She took the children and all the silver."
"Please don't hurt me! I have nothing of value."
"Take whatever you want! Just spare my family!"
"I'll do anything — just don't burn our fields."
"The gods have abandoned us. First plague, now bandits."
"Run! Run to the hills! They're coming!"
"I can't fight. I'm just a simple farmer."
"My children are hiding in the shed. Please don't find them."
"I never hurt anyone. Why is this happening?"
"Let me go and I'll never speak of this."
"The soldiers are worse than the bandits. They take everything."
merchant: "Here's my purse. All of it. Just let me go."
"I'll pay triple next season if you spare my warehouse."
"Don't kill me! I have connections — they'll ransom me well!"
"The guards will be here any moment. You should leave."
"I have a family. Please, think of them."
"Take my goods but leave my ledgers. They're worthless to you."
"I always pay protection money. This shouldn't be happening."
"I'll give you credit at any shop in the province. Just name it."
"If you kill me, my debtors will never pay. You'll lose money."
"I'll sign a contract giving you half my profits. Please."
samurai: "Do your worst. I will not dishonour my clan."
"If I die, my lord will avenge me. You gain nothing."
"I have faced worse than you on the battlefield."
"My sword is not for sale, bandit. Come and take it."
"Kill me if you must, but spare the villagers."
"You think fear will break me? I've stared death in the face before."
"My ancestors watch me. I will not shame them."
"Cut me down, but you'll never see your homeland again."
"I've written my death poem already. I'm ready."
"A samurai does not beg. Do what you came to do."

commoner: "How dare you speak to me that way!"
"Get off my land before I set the dogs on you!"
"I've had enough of outsiders causing trouble here."
"You think you're better than us? Leave now."
"One more word and I'll fetch the constable."
"I'll remember your face. You'll regret this."
"Touch my family and I'll kill you myself."
"You're not welcome here. Turn around and go."
"I've dealt with worse than you. Don't test me."
"You've insulted my house. An apology won't be enough."
samurai: "You insult my honour. Draw your blade or apologise."
"I'll cut you down where you stand."
"Nobody speaks to a {clan} retainer like that."
"You're either brave or foolish. We'll soon see which."
"A duel, then. Sunrise, outside the shrine."
"I've killed men for less. Consider this your final warning."
"My patience is as thin as a blade's edge."
"Your words are an insult to my entire bloodline."
"I don't fight cowards. Prove you're worth my time."
"You'll kneel before I cut you down."
merchant: "You try to cheat me? I'll ruin your reputation in every province!"
"Guards! This scoundrel is trying to use counterfeit coin!"
"I didn't build my trade by being intimidated. Get out of my shop."
"You owe me money, and I always collect."
"Never darken my doorstep again, or I'll have you arrested."
"Do you know who my creditors are? They could buy your village."
"I can make one call and have you blacklisted from every port."
"You think you can out‑haggle me? I was bargaining while you were in diapers."
"Take your business elsewhere. I don't deal with cheats."
"If you cross me, you'll find your shipments mysteriously lost."

drought: "The wells are drying up. If it doesn't rain soon, the crops will fail."
"The river is lower than I've ever seen. Even the fish are dying."
"The daimyō ordered prayers at every shrine, but the sky remains clear."
"Rice prices are going to soar. Mark my words."
flood: "The Tone River burst its banks. Half the village is underwater."
"We lost everything — the house, the tools, the winter grain."
"Bodies washed up downstream. The water shows no mercy."
"They're calling it the worst flood in a generation."
bandit_raid: "A caravan was hit two days ago. No survivors."
"The bandits have been seen near the pass. Travellers are warned."
"They came at night. Took the grain and burned what they couldn't carry."
"The magistrate is posting a bounty, but it's not enough."
war: "Troops are marching east. The Shogunate has called the banners."
"A siege has begun at a castle in Musashi. We heard the cannons."
"Refugees are pouring into town. They say their village was razed."
"The war will be over by winter — or so the daimyō claims."
festival: "The streets are decorated for the harvest festival. Finally, some joy!"
"There's going to be a sumo match in the square tomorrow."
"Fireworks! The temple is setting off fireworks for the first time in years."
"Sake is half price during the festival. My favourite time of year."
supernatural: "A ghost was seen at the crossroads. They say it's a woman who died in childbirth."
"A strange light moves through the forest at night. The monks say it's a hitodama."
"A fortune teller arrived yesterday. She predicted a terrible earthquake."
"The shrine maiden heard the voice of the kami in a dream. She's been silent ever since."

blacksmith: "This steel is from the Satake mines. Harder than anything you'll find in the south."
"I can forge you a blade that will never fail in battle. It will cost you, though."
"The secret to a good edge is in the quenching. Water or oil — that's the question."
"My master taught me the old techniques. I'm one of the few left."
"If you bring me raw iron, I'll give you a discount on the work."
"Quenching is an art. Too fast, the blade shatters. Too slow, it's soft."
"A katana is not just a weapon; it's the soul of the samurai. I treat each blade as a prayer."
"I don't just make weapons. Nails, hinges, cooking pots — all essential."
"See these bellows? They've been in my family for three generations."
"The iron sand from the mountains is the best. It folds a hundred times without breaking."
carpenter: "See this joint? No nails needed. That's the art of the daiku."
"I built the new temple gate. Took me three months of carving."
"Good timber is getting harder to find. The forest is thinning."
"I can make you a chest that will last a hundred years. Or longer."
"Every beam I cut is measured against the morning sun. Precision is everything."
"A house built right will stand through earthquakes. The secret is in the foundation stones."
"I use cypress for the frames — it resists rot and insects."
"The daimyō's palace has sliding doors I made. They still glide like silk."
"If you need a bridge, I can span a river in a week."
"My tools are my children. I sharpen them every morning before work."
fisherman: "Today's catch was all bonito. The market will be flooded."
"You have to read the currents. The fish follow them like roads."
"My grandfather told me about a great whale that lives in Sagami Bay."
"Nets need mending every week. Salt water eats through everything."
"The best fishing is at dawn, when the sea is glass."
"We offer prayers to Ebisu-sama before every voyage."
"I once pulled up a fish with a pearl in its belly. Sold it for a year's wages."
"A storm can come out of nowhere. Always keep an eye on the clouds."
"Cormorant fishing? That's for the wealthy to watch. I use a net."
"Seaweed harvest is in spring. The young shoots are the most tender."
healer: "For fever, boil willow bark and drink the tea. It's bitter but effective."
"This herb poultice will draw out the infection. Change it twice a day."
"Acupuncture can relieve pain, but you must trust the needles."
"I learned my art from a Chinese physician who passed through years ago."
"Ginger root warms the blood. Chew it raw for a cold."
"Moxibustion — burning mugwort on the skin — can cure deep ailments."
"A calm mind heals faster than any medicine. Meditate daily."
"If the wound is festering, clean it with sake and apply honey."
"I treat all who come, rich or poor. Payment is whatever you can give."
"Some illnesses are caused by angry spirits. In those cases, call a priest."
potter: "This clay is from Seto — the finest in the land. It fires to a warm, rich colour."
"A good bowl should feel like it belongs in your hands. It's not just a vessel; it's a companion."
"I can make a tea cup so thin you can see the shadow of the tea through it."
"Kiln temperature must be perfect. Too hot and everything cracks."
"Each piece is glazed with ash from the rice fields. No two are alike."
"I studied under a master in Shigaraki. He taught me that imperfections are beauty."
"A cracked pot can be mended with lacquer and gold — it tells a story."
"The tea ceremony masters demand perfection. One mistake, and they reject the whole set."
weaver: "This silk is from our own silkworms. The thread is stronger than it looks."
"Indigo dyeing is a messy business, but the colour lasts a lifetime."
"I can weave a pattern so complex it tells an entire story."
"A well‑made kimono should move with the body, not against it."
"Cotton is for everyday; silk is for special occasions. Hemp is for work."
"I use a shuttle made of cherry wood. It's smooth and never snags."
"The daimyō's wife ordered a bolt of red silk. It took me a month."
"If you bring me raw silk, I can weave it into cloth in three days."
"Weaving is meditation. The rhythm of the loom clears the mind."
"A torn garment can be rewoven so well you'd never see the mend."
stonemason: "Granite from the mountains is best for castle walls. It laughs at catapults."
"I carved the guardian statues at the shrine. They've stood for fifty years."
"A good foundation stone must be perfectly flat. I use a level and patience."
"Stone remembers every blow of the chisel. You can't rush it."
"Limestone is easier to carve but weathers faster. Choose wisely."
"I learned from my uncle, who built the great bridge over the Tone River."
"A stone lantern properly placed will stand for centuries."
"Gravestones are the most important work. They carry a family's name forever."
lacquerer: "This sap is from the urushi tree. It hardens to a mirror finish."
"Lacquerware must be applied in thin layers, one per day, for weeks."
"The red pigment comes from cinnabar. It's rare and expensive."
"A well‑made lacquer bowl can hold boiling soup without harming the hands."
"Gold leaf applied over lacquer creates a design worthy of a palace."
"The poison in raw lacquer gives a terrible rash. I've suffered many times."
"Each piece takes months to finish. That's why it commands such a price."
paper_maker: "Washi paper is made from mulberry bark. It's stronger than it looks."
"A good sheet of paper can last a thousand years if kept dry."
"I supply the temple with paper for sutras. They demand the best."
"The mould and deckle are my most prized tools. They're worn smooth by years of use."
"White paper is for poetry; cream paper is for letters. Each has its place."
"I can make paper so thin you can see the moon through it."

offer: "I need {itemName} urgently. My {familyMember} is ill and the healer says this will help. Can you find it?"
"If you could bring me {itemName}, I'd be in your debt. The market is out, and the next caravan won't come for weeks."
"A simple task for a capable person: fetch me {itemName} from {location}. I'll pay well."
"My workshop is useless without {itemName}. I can't craft anything until I get more. Please, I'll make it worth your while."
"I'm looking for {itemName}. None of the local merchants have it. You seem resourceful — can you help?"
"My son is getting married, and we need {itemName} for the ceremony. I'll pay extra for speed."
"A traveller like you must know the roads. Bring me {itemName} from {location} and earn my gratitude."
"The temple requires {itemName} for a ritual. The abbot will reward you handsomely."
"I've searched everywhere. If you can find {itemName}, I'll give you something valuable in return."
"There's good coin in it if you can procure {itemName}. I'm desperate."
accept: "Thank you! You're a lifesaver. I'll prepare your reward."
"May the kami guide your steps. I'll wait here."
"Excellent. Remember, I need {itemName}, not a substitute. The quality matters."
"Good. I knew you had an honest face. Don't be long — it's urgent."
"I'll write down the details so you don't forget. Take this note."
"Be careful on the roads. Bandits have been spotted lately."
"If anyone asks, you're on an errand for the village headman."
"Take this small advance. It's all I can spare until you return."
"The sooner you're back, the better. But safety first."
"I'll pray for your swift return."
remind: "Any luck finding {itemName} yet? I'm getting anxious."
"I haven't forgotten our deal. I hope you haven't either."
"Still waiting on that {itemName}. My need grows each day."
"No rush… well, actually, yes rush. But I trust you."
"The healer stopped by again. The medicine is urgently needed."
"Every day without {itemName} is a day my {familyMember} suffers."
"Have you checked the market in {location}? They might have it."
"I can offer a little more coin if that speeds things up."
"Don't forget me. I'm counting on you."
"If you can't find it, come back anyway. We'll think of something."
complete: "You found it! I can't thank you enough. Here's your reward, as promised."
"Perfect, just what I needed. You're a true friend."
"Excellent quality! This will do nicely. Take this for your trouble."
"Finally! I was starting to lose hope. You've earned this."
"My {familyMember} is saved because of you. I'll never forget this."
"The temple will bless you for your help. Here's the abbot's donation."
"Wonderful! I can finish my work now. Please, accept this bonus."
"You're a miracle worker. I'll spread word of your reliability."
"I knew I could trust you. If you ever need a favour, just ask."
"Here, take a little extra. It's not much, but it comes from the heart."

offer: "A {beast} has been terrorising our village. It killed three of our goats and wounded a child. Please, kill it before it takes a life."
"You look capable with that blade. There's a bounty on a {beast} that's been haunting the {location} forest. Interested?"
"The magistrate posted a reward for anyone who slays the {beast}. But I'm adding my own coin on top. The creature must die."
"My hunting party was slaughtered. I'm the only survivor. That {beast} is no ordinary animal. Will you help?"
"A monstrous {beast} has been seen near the rice paddies. It's scared the farmers so much they won't go out. We need a warrior."
"The {beast} came down from the mountains last week. It's been killing livestock every night. The village is in terror."
"I'll pay you handsomely if you can bring me the head of the {beast}. My son was its first victim."
"The shrine maiden says the {beast} is a punishment from the kami. But punishment or not, we need it gone."
"No one else is brave enough. They say you've killed before. Prove them right."
"There's a cave in the hills where the {beast} lairs. Bring torches and a strong arm."
accept: "Buddha bless you. Be careful — it's smart, not just strong."
"I'll pray for your success. Bring back its head as proof."
"Take this charm. It's blessed by the shrine maiden. May it protect you."
"We'll all be cheering for you. Don't underestimate it."
"The {beast} is most active at dawn. Catch it unawares."
"Use fire — it's afraid of flames. At least, that's what the hunters say."
"Its hide is thick. Aim for the eyes or the throat."
"I'll have a feast prepared for your return. Come back alive."
"Don't go alone if you can help it. There's strength in numbers."
"My daughter made you these rice balls. They're for luck."
remind: "The {beast} struck again last night. We're running out of time."
"Have you tracked it yet? Every day we wait, it grows bolder."
"I've gathered more coin for the reward. Please, don't abandon us."
"The children are terrified to leave their homes. You're our only hope."
"I heard it howling again. It sounded closer this time."
"The village headman is getting impatient. He's talking about hiring mercenaries."
"One of the farmers tried to hunt it himself. He's in the healer's hut now."
"If you need anything — weapons, traps, poisons — just ask."
"The moon is full tonight. The {beast} will be out. It's your best chance."
"Please hurry. My wife cries herself to sleep every night."
complete: "You killed it! The whole village is celebrating! Here's the bounty, and my eternal thanks."
"That monster's head will hang at the village gate as a warning to all beasts. You've saved us."
"I watched from a distance. Your skill with {weapon} is remarkable. The reward is yours."
"We can finally sleep without fear. Thank you, brave warrior."
"You've done what no one else could. The magistrate will hear of your deed."
"My daughter wants to thank you personally. She's alive because of you."
"The village has named a street after you. It's a small honour, but heartfelt."
"Take this, on top of the reward. It's my grandfather's lucky stone. It kept him safe in many battles."
"There will be songs sung about you tonight. You're a hero."
"If you ever need a place to stay, my home is yours."

offer: "I need to get word to my brother in {destination}. It's too dangerous for me. Would you carry a letter?"
"This message must reach the garrison commander before dawn. The enemy plans to attack. Will you be my courier?"
"My daughter married a merchant in {destination}. I haven't heard from her in months. Take this letter, and I'll pay you well."
"The information in this scroll could prevent a war. But I can't leave my post. You — take it to {destination}."
"A secret message for the daimyō's advisor. If you're caught, destroy it. Are you willing?"
"I'm too old for the road, but this letter must reach my son. He's a soldier stationed in {destination}."
"The priest said this sutra must be delivered to a dying monk. It's a matter of the spirit."
"A love letter. I'm too shy to take it myself. Can you hand it to the innkeeper's daughter in {destination}?"
"This scroll contains trade negotiations. If it falls into the wrong hands, my business is ruined."
"I'll make it worth your while. Deliver this to the temple in {destination} and ask for the head priest."
accept: "Remember: deliver it into his hands only. No one else can read it."
"Travel swiftly. The road is dangerous, but I sense you can handle it."
"If anyone tries to take it from you, destroy it first. The contents are sensitive."
"May the wind be at your back. I'll await word of your success."
"Here's a seal — it proves you're my messenger. Show it only at the gate."
"Take the northern path; it's less travelled but safer."
"If you're stopped by guards, say you're carrying temple correspondence."
"I'll give you half now, half when you return. Fair?"
"Don't stop at inns. Sleep by the road if you must. Time is critical."
"Return with a reply, and I'll double your pay."
remind: "You haven't left yet? Every hour counts!"
"I'm counting on you. Don't let me down."
"Has the letter been delivered? I'm on edge waiting for news."
"If you've lost the letter, tell me now. I'll find another way."
"The situation has worsened. If you haven't left, please hurry."
"I had a nightmare that you were captured. Please be careful."
"My brother's life depends on this. I'm begging you."
"If you meet trouble, remember: the letter is more important than your life."
"I've prayed at the shrine for your safe passage."
"Any word from {destination}? I can't sleep."
complete: "You delivered it? And he read it? Excellent! Here's what I promised."
"You've done a great service. This will change many fates. Thank you."
"Safe and sound. I knew I could trust you. Your payment."
"The response arrived this morning. Your help was invaluable."
"Because of you, a war was avoided. I can't tell you more, but the daimyō is grateful."
"My daughter sent a letter back! She's alive and well. Thank you, thank you."
"The dying monk received the sutra and passed peacefully. The temple blesses you."
"Here's a little extra. Your speed was remarkable."
"You handled it like a professional. I'll use your services again."
"You're more than a courier — you're a saviour. Take this, with my deepest thanks."

offer: "I must travel to {destination} for my sister's funeral, but the road is full of bandits. Escort me, and I'll pay."
"My master, a wealthy merchant, needs safe passage to {destination}. He travels with valuable goods. Will you guard him?"
"There's a convoy leaving at dawn. We need swords to protect it. Pay is generous, danger is real."
"I'm transporting temple treasures to a new shrine. The monks have blessed the journey, but blessings don't stop arrows."
"A noblewoman needs to visit her husband's grave in {destination}. She cannot travel alone."
"Three wagons of rice are heading to {destination}. Bandits know the route. We need guards."
"A group of pilgrims seek an escort through the mountain pass. They'll pay what they can."
"My daughter is to be married in {destination}. I must deliver her safely, and her dowry."
"I'm a tax collector carrying silver. If I'm robbed, the daimyō will have my head. Protect me."
"A cartographer wants to map the old forest road. He needs a fighter by his side."
accept: "We leave at first light. Stay close to the wagons."
"If bandits attack, protect the cargo first. The horses second. Me third."
"I hope we don't need your blade, but I'm glad it's with us."
"Keep your eyes on the treeline. That's where they always hide."
"I'll provide food and drink for the journey. Your sword is all I ask."
"Stay quiet and don't draw attention. We want to pass unnoticed."
"If we meet soldiers, let me do the talking."
"We travel by moonlight to avoid the heat. You'll get used to it."
"The pay is good, but the road is cursed. Still in?"
"Glad to have you. Your reputation precedes you."
remind: "The caravan is almost ready. Are you prepared?"
"We can't afford delays. The pass might be snowed in if we wait."
"I dreamed of wolves last night. Stay alert."
"The other guards are nervous. They look to you for confidence."
"One of the horses is lame. Can you help find a replacement?"
"A scout says there are fresh tracks ahead. Bandits, probably."
"The merchants are complaining about the pace. We need to move faster."
"The pilgrims want to stop at every shrine. It's slowing us down."
"The noblewoman insists on riding in a palanquin. It's a target."
"We're halfway there. The hardest part is behind us."
complete: "We made it! Not a single scratch on the cargo. You're worth every mon."
"Your vigilance saved us at the river crossing. I'll recommend you to every merchant I know."
"Safe at last. Here's your payment, plus a bonus for your courage."
"That was the smoothest journey I've had in years. You're a born protector."
"The temple is grateful. They've inscribed your name in the shrine records."
"My daughter is safely married. You're invited to the feast."
"The daimyō's silver is secure. He may have more work for you."
"The pilgrims are singing your praises. They said you were a guardian angel."
"The map is complete. The cartographer says he'll name a road after you."
"I've never felt safer on the road. Thank you, friend."

taunt: "I've cut down stronger men before breakfast."
"Your stance is sloppy. Did you learn swordsmanship from a fishwife?"
"I'll send you to meet your ancestors."
"Drop your weapon and I might let you walk away."
"Is that blade for show, or do you actually know how to use it?"
"Your mother would weep to see you now."
"Come, let's see if you bleed like a man or squeal like a pig."
"This will be over quickly. Don't blink."
"I've been itching for a fight. Thank you for volunteering."
player_win: "You fought well. Rest now — your battle is over."
"The ground drinks your blood. I hope your cause was worth it."
"Another corpse for the crows. May you find peace in the next life."
"I take no joy in this. But you left me no choice."
"Your sword will be well used. Your body, left for the wolves."
enemy_yield: "Wait! I yield! Please, I have a family!"
"Enough! Spare me, and I'll give you everything I have."
"I can't fight anymore. My arm is numb. Mercy!"
"You're better than me. I admit it. Just let me go."
"I'll leave this province and never return. I swear it on my ancestors."
enemy_flee: "You're a demon! Stay away from me!"
"I'll remember your face. Next time, I'll be ready."
"The whole clan will hear of this!"

samurai: "A worthy... end... my ancestors... welcome me..."
"My lord... I have failed... the {clan} name..."
"Remember me... when you face... the next blade..."
merchant: "My wealth... take it... just... let my family know..."
"All my trading... ends here... in the dirt..."
commoner: "Please... my children..."
"I never wanted... any of this..."

drought: "The earth is cracking. I've never seen a season this dry."
"We pray to the dragon king, but the sky gives nothing."
"At this rate, we'll be eating bark by winter."
flood: "The river swallowed the bridge. There's no crossing now."
"Mud everywhere. It'll take weeks to clean up."
"I lost two chickens to the flood. Could've been worse."
bandit_raid: "I heard screams last night. I barred my door and prayed."
"They say the bandits have a camp in the mountains. Someone should do something."
"The magistrate's useless. We need samurai, not tax collectors."
war: "The soldiers took half our grain. Said it was for the war effort."
"My son was conscripted. I may never see him again."
"The cannons echo for miles. Even the birds have fled."
festival: "Have you tried the sweet dumplings? My wife made them."
"Even a poor man can smile during a festival."
"Drink up! Tomorrow we go back to reality."
supernatural: "I'm not superstitious, but I've been avoiding the crossroads at night."
"The priest says it's a hungry ghost. An offering might appease it."
"My dog refuses to go near the old well. Animals know things."

monk: "Life is like a dewdrop on a leaf — beautiful and brief."
"Attachment is the root of suffering. Let go of what you cannot keep."
"The cherry blossom falls without regret. So should we."
"In silence, you can hear the universe breathe."
"You are not your thoughts. You are the one who observes them."
samurai: "The way of the warrior is to accept death."
"A true samurai needs only his sword and his honour."
"Victory is not in the killing, but in the not needing to kill."
"Discipline is freedom. Chaos is slavery."
"If you fear death, you are already dead."
merchant: "Time is money. Every idle moment is a lost opportunity."
"A good reputation is worth more than gold."
"Only fools chase profit without considering risk."
"The best deal leaves both sides satisfied."
"Markets rise and fall, but a wise merchant endures."
farmer: "You reap what you sow. That's the first law of the land."
"Patience is the farmer's greatest tool."
"The land doesn't lie. If you care for it, it cares for you."
"We're all just passing through. The fields will be here long after we're gone."
"Hunger teaches what no book can."

commoner: "Safe travels. May the road rise to meet you."
"Come back anytime. Our door is always open."
"Kami watch over you."
"Don't be a stranger."
"Until we meet again."
merchant: "Pleasure doing business. Tell your friends about my shop."
"Next time you're in town, I might have new stock."
"Here's a little something for the road. On the house."
"Farewell, and may your purse stay heavy."
monk: "Go in peace. The Buddha walks with you."
"Remember: the journey is the destination."
"If you ever need guidance, the temple gate is never locked."
samurai: "Until we cross swords again — or not."
"Stay sharp. The world is a battlefield."
"If our paths cross again, I hope it's as allies."
